description:
To set the runtime status of the kernel.perf_cpu_time_max_percent kernel parameter, run the following command: 
$ sudo sysctl -w kernel.perf_cpu_time_max_percent=1
To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: 
kernel.perf_cpu_time_max_percent = 1
rationale:
The <tt>kernel.perf_cpu_time_max_percent</tt> configures a threshold of
maximum percentile of CPU that can be used by Perf system. Restricting usage
of <tt>Perf</tt> system decreases risk of potential availability problems.
ocil_clause: the correct value is not returned
ocil:
The runtime status of the kernel.perf_cpu_time_max_percent kernel parameter can be queried by running the following command: 
$ sysctl kernel.perf_cpu_time_max_percent
1.
title: Limit CPU consumption of the Perf system
